trade.summary: Very strong bilateral trade between two of Europe's most open economies. Paesi Bassi exports chemicals, refined petroleum, agricultural products, machinery, and semiconductor equipment (ASML); Svezia exports vehicles (Volvo, Scania), machinery, pharmaceuticals (AstraZeneca), paper, and wood products. Rotterdam is the primary European port for Swedish imports and exports. Dutch and Swedish multinationals are deeply intertwined through supply chains.

diplomatic.summary: Excellent bilateral relations. Both are liberal democracies with strong multilateralist orientations. Svezia's accession to NATO in 2024 brought Svezia into the same alliance as the Paesi Bassi, further cementing security ties. Both countries are close on EU policy, particularly on trade openness, Ucraina support, and rule-of-law enforcement. Regular high-level consultations on EU and NATO issues.

military.summary: NATO allies since Svezia's 2024 accession. Both countries support Ucraina with military aid. Paesi Bassi and Svezia cooperate on air defense, maritime security in the Baltic and North Seas, and intelligence sharing within NATO frameworks.

financial.summary: Deep bilateral investment. Svezia is a top-10 FDI partner of the Paesi Bassi and vice versa. ASML and Ericsson have major cross-country business ties. Swedish pension funds hold Dutch equities; Dutch pension funds hold Swedish equities. Amsterdam-Stockholm financial corridor is active.

energy.summary: Paesi Bassi supplies Svezia with refined petroleum products through Rotterdam. Both countries are transitioning to renewable energy; cooperation on offshore wind in the North Sea is significant. Svezia exports hydropower-based electricity to Nordic grid neighbors.

scientific.summary: Very active scientific cooperation. ASML-Ericsson supply chain research, Karolinska-AMC medical research collaboration, and extensive EU Horizon Europe joint projects. Both countries are among Europe's top research performers and maintain extensive university exchange programs.
